valid.name: identity-column
valid.code: CREATE TABLE t (id INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY);

valid.name: never-serial
valid.code: CREATE TABLE t (id bigserial PRIMARY KEY);

errors.message: Use `GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY` (SQL standard) instead of `bigserial`. The serial pseudo-types create a separately-owned sequence that breaks under pg_dump round-trips and does not honor column privileges.

invalid.name: never-identity
invalid.code: CREATE TABLE t (id bigint GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY);

errors.message: Use a serial pseudo-type (e.g. `bigserial`) instead of `GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY`. Useful for projects that need to keep compatibility with tooling that does not understand identity columns.

Use `GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY` (SQL standard) instead of `serial`. The serial pseudo-types create a separately-owned sequence that breaks under pg_dump round-trips and does not honor column privileges.

Use `GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY` (SQL standard) instead of `smallserial`. The serial pseudo-types create a separately-owned sequence that breaks under pg_dump round-trips and does not honor column privileges.
